numeral.purpose: Grid digit and keypad key; weight encodes authorship (700 user, 400 solver), tabular figures.

body.purpose: Subtitle, status line, and the Why-17 explanation.

shadows.name: frame-lift
shadows.purpose: The board frame's only shadow; a faint ambient resting elevation, never over 8px blur.

motion.name: state-transition
motion.purpose: Color-only transitions for selection, conflict, solved, hover, and keypad-press states. Ease-out, 150ms.

motion.name: why-reveal
motion.purpose: Fade-and-rise entrance for the Why-17 disclosure panel. The only non-instant reveal in the system.

motion.name: reduced-motion
motion.purpose: Under prefers-reduced-motion: reduce, all transitions and animations collapse to instant.

components.name: Primary Button
components.description: The Solve action. The one filled, brand-colored button on the page. Enter is its keyboard shortcut while the grid has focus.
components.css: .ds-btn-primary { background: var(--primary); color: var(--primary-fg); font-family: Inter, system-ui, sans-serif; font-size: 1rem; font-weight: 500; line-height: 1.5rem; padding: 8px 20px; border: none; border-radius: 8px; cursor: pointer; transition: opacity 150ms cubic-bezier(0,0,0.2,1); } .ds-btn-primary:hover { opacity: 0.9; } .ds-btn-primary:focus-visible { outline: none; box-shadow: 0 0 0 2px var(--bg), 0 0 0 4px var(--primary); }

components.name: Secondary Button
components.description: The Clear action. Outlined, recedes behind the primary. A non-empty Clear is undoable via the status line.
components.css: .ds-btn-secondary { background: var(--bg); color: var(--ink); font-family: Inter, system-ui, sans-serif; font-size: 1rem; font-weight: 500; line-height: 1.5rem; padding: 8px 20px; border: 1px solid var(--grid-line); border-radius: 8px; cursor: pointer; transition: background-color 150ms cubic-bezier(0,0,0.2,1); } .ds-btn-secondary:hover { background: var(--surface); } .ds-btn-secondary:focus-visible { outline: none; box-shadow: 0 0 0 2px var(--bg), 0 0 0 4px var(--primary); }

components.name: Sudoku Cell
components.description: The signature element. One square cell with default, selected, related, conflict, and solved states. Authorship shows in weight as well as color. The grid is a roving-tabindex composite: only the selected cell is in the tab order.
components.css: .ds-cellrow { display: inline-flex; border: 2px solid var(--grid-line-bold); border-radius: 12px; overflow: hidden; background: var(--bg); } .ds-cell { width: 3rem; height: 3rem; display: flex; align-items: center; justify-content: center; background: var(--bg); color: var(--ink); font-family: Inter, system-ui, sans-serif; font-size: 1.5rem; font-weight: 700; font-variant-numeric: tabular-nums; border: none; border-right: 1px solid var(--grid-line); cursor: pointer; transition: background-color 150ms cubic-bezier(0,0,0.2,1); } .ds-cell:last-child { border-right: none; } .ds-cell--related { background: var(--cell-related); } .ds-cell--solved { color: var(--accent); font-weight: 400; } .ds-cell--conflict { background: var(--cell-conflict-bg); color: var(--danger); text-decoration: underline; text-decoration-thickness: 2px; text-underline-offset: 2px; } .ds-cell--selected { background: var(--primary); color: var(--primary-fg); }

components.name: Number Keypad
components.description: Touch-only digit entry, shown for coarse pointers so the desktop layout stays minimal. Keys 1-9 plus an erase key write to the selected cell, the same path as typing a digit.
components.css: .ds-keypad { display: grid; grid-template-columns: repeat(5, 1fr); gap: 6px; width: min(90vw, 30rem); } .ds-key { display: flex; align-items: center; justify-content: center; min-height: 3rem; background: var(--bg); color: var(--ink); font-family: Inter, system-ui, sans-serif; font-size: 1.25rem; font-weight: 600; font-variant-numeric: tabular-nums; border: 1px solid var(--grid-line); border-radius: 12px; cursor: pointer; transition: background-color 150ms cubic-bezier(0,0,0.2,1); } .ds-key:hover { background: var(--surface); } .ds-key:active { background: var(--cell-related); } .ds-key:focus-visible { outline: none; box-shadow: inset 0 0 0 2px var(--primary); }

components.name: Status Line
components.description: A single reserved line for the solve verdict. Holds its height when empty so the layout never jumps, and wraps to host one inline text action: a Why-17 disclosure on the clue-floor warning, or Undo after a Clear or paste.
components.html: Enter at least 17 clues to solve (you have 9)
components.css: .ds-status { display: flex; flex-wrap: wrap; align-items: center; justify-content: center; gap: 0 8px; min-height: 1.5rem; margin: 0; font-family: Inter, system-ui, sans-serif; font-size: 0.875rem; font-weight: 500; } .ds-status--info { color: var(--muted); } .ds-status--warning { color: var(--warning); } .ds-status--error { color: var(--danger); } .ds-status--success { color: var(--accent); } .ds-status-action { background: none; border: none; padding: 0; color: var(--primary); font: inherit; text-decoration: underline; text-underline-offset: 2px; cursor: pointer; } .ds-status-action:hover { opacity: 0.8; }

narrative.overview: A well-made instrument, not an app with personality to spend. A person arrives with a puzzle, types it in, and gets a correct verdict in seconds. The interface earns trust the way a good ruler or calculator does: exact, quiet, and disappearing into the task. One committed brand color (Layout Blue) carries selection, the primary action, and focus against deliberately neutral surfaces; warmth comes from the color and the type, never from a tinted background. Color never carries meaning alone: user and solver digits differ in weight as well as hue, and conflicts carry an underline as well as red. Input is device-aware (hardware keyboard on desktop, an on-screen keypad on touch), and every board-replacing action is recoverable with a single inline Undo.
keyCharacteristics: One committed brand color; neutral surfaces.
Authorship is unmistakable: user digits are bold ink, solver digits are normal-weight teal.
Redundant encoding: every color meaning has a second, non-chromatic cue.
Auto light/dark, WCAG AA, full keyboard operation.
Input is device-aware: hardware keyboard on desktop, an on-screen keypad on touch.
Every board-replacing action (Clear, paste) is recoverable with a single Undo.

rules.name: The Authorship Rule
rules.body: A user-entered digit and a solver-filled digit must never be told apart by color alone. User digits are Ink at weight 700; solver digits are Solver Teal at weight 400. Hue and weight always move together.

rules.name: The Neutral Surface Rule
rules.body: The body and cell background is pure white (or near-black in dark mode). Warmth and coolness live in the brand color and the type, never in a tinted surface. Cream, sand, and beige backgrounds are prohibited.

rules.name: The Auto-Theme Rule
rules.body: Every token ships a light and a dark value, switched by prefers-color-scheme. There is no manual theme toggle; the system follows the OS.

rules.name: The Tabular Rule
rules.body: Grid digits always use font-variant-numeric: tabular-nums. A 1 and a 9 occupy the same width so the grid never shifts as it fills.

rules.name: The Flat Frame Rule
rules.body: Surfaces are flat. The board carries one whisper-light ambient shadow; everything else is flat and reads through color, border, and weight. No element pairs a 1px border with a wide soft shadow.

rules.name: The Single-Selection Rule
rules.body: Exactly one cell is selected and DOM focus is always on it. Navigation moves both together; they never diverge.

rules.name: The Recoverable-Replace Rule
rules.body: Any action that replaces the whole board (Clear, paste) is undoable inline. Destructive board changes never use a confirmation dialog.

rules.name: The Scoped-Keys Rule
rules.body: Grid keystrokes (digits, arrows, Enter) are handled on the grid, not the window, so focus determines a key's meaning. Paste is the one global listener, because it has no single focus target.

dos: Do carry every color meaning with a second cue: weight for authorship, underline for conflict. Color alone is never the signal.
Do keep the body and cell background pure white in light mode and near-black in dark mode; let Layout Blue and the type carry the character.
Do keep grid digits on tabular-nums so columns stay aligned.
Do state verdicts plainly: "No solution exists", "Multiple solutions exist; showing one", "Solved". No exclamation, no celebration.
Do keep Solver Teal at hue ~200 so it stays separable from Conflict Red for red-green color-blind users.
Do make every board-replacing action (Clear, paste) undoable inline, and keep DOM focus on the selected cell so it never drifts from the selection.
Do gate the keypad to touch (pointer: coarse); desktop relies on the hardware keyboard so the layout stays minimal.
donts: Don't ship the toy/gamey register: no confetti, stars, streaks, or cartoon styling on a solve.
Don't drift toward the ad-heavy "free sudoku solver" look: cluttered, slow, untrustworthy chrome around the grid.
Don't use the generic AI-default surface: warm cream/beige backgrounds, a dusty accent, soft drop-shadowed cards, or an uppercase tracked eyebrow over a section.
Don't pair a 1px border with a wide soft shadow (the ghost-card tell), or round cards past 16px.
Don't distinguish solver digits from user digits by color only, and never signal a conflict with red text alone.
Don't add a confirmation modal for Clear or paste; use the inline Undo. Modals are not part of this system.
Don't make the grid 81 tab stops or let DOM focus and selection diverge; the grid is one roving-tabindex stop.
